rule: Shanghai Stock Exchange Trading Rules (Revised in 2023)
rule_cn: Shanghai Stock Exchange Trading Rules (Revised in 2023)

rule: Chapter 1 General Provisions
rule_cn: Chapter 1 General Provisions

rule: 1.1 This rule is formulated to regulate securities market trading activities, maintain securities market order, and protect the legitimate rights and interests of investors, in accordance with the Securities Law of the People's Republic of China, the Administrative Measures for Securities Exchanges, and other laws, administrative regulations, departmental rules, normative documents (hereinafter collectively referred to as laws and regulations) and the Articles of Association of the Shanghai Stock Exchange.
rule_cn: 1.1 This rule is formulated to regulate securities market trading activities, maintain securities market order, and protect the legitimate rights and interests of investors, in accordance with the Securities Law of the People's Republic of China, the Administrative Measures for Securities Exchanges, and other laws, administrative regulations, departmental rules, normative documents (hereinafter collectively referred to as laws and regulations) and the Articles of Association of the Shanghai Stock Exchange.

rule: 1.2 This rule applies to the trading of stocks, funds, warrants, depositary receipts, and other trading instruments approved by the China Securities Regulatory Commission (hereinafter referred to as CSRC) listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange (hereinafter referred to as the Exchange). Where this rule does not provide for a specific matter, other relevant regulations of the Exchange shall apply. Trading of depositary receipts shall be subject to the relevant regulations for A-share trading. Where the Exchange has separate regulations for trading depositary receipts, preferred shares, publicly offered infrastructure investment funds, etc., those regulations shall prevail.
rule_cn: 1.2 This rule applies to the trading of stocks, funds, warrants, depositary receipts, and other trading instruments approved by the China Securities Regulatory Commission (hereinafter referred to as CSRC) listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange (hereinafter referred to as the Exchange). Where this rule does not provide for a specific matter, other relevant regulations of the Exchange shall apply. Trading of depositary receipts shall be subject to the relevant regulations for A-share trading. Where the Exchange has separate regulations for trading depositary receipts, preferred shares, publicly offered infrastructure investment funds, etc., those regulations shall prevail.

rule: 1.3 Securities trading shall adhere to the principles of openness, fairness, and impartiality.
rule_cn: 1.3 Securities trading shall adhere to the principles of openness, fairness, and impartiality.

rule: 1.4 Securities transactions shall comply with laws, administrative regulations, departmental rules, and the relevant business rules of this exchange, and shall adhere to the principles of voluntariness, compensation, and good faith.
rule_cn: 1.4 Securities trading shall comply with laws, administrative regulations, departmental rules, and the relevant business rules of this exchange, and shall adhere to the principles of voluntariness, compensation, and good faith.

rule: 1.5 Securities trading shall be conducted through paperless centralized trading or other methods approved by the China Securities Regulatory Commission.
rule_cn: 1.5 Securities trading shall be conducted through paperless centralized trading or other methods approved by the China Securities Regulatory Commission.

rule: Chapter Two: Trading Markets
rule_cn: Chapter Two: Trading Markets

rule: Section 1 Trading Venue
rule_cn: Section 1 Trading Venues

rule: 2.1.1 This exchange provides trading venues and facilities for securities trading. These venues and facilities consist of a trading mainframe, trading floor, participant trading units, order placement system, and related communication systems, etc.
rule_cn: 2.1.1 This exchange provides trading venues and facilities for securities trading. These venues and facilities consist of a trading mainframe, trading floor, participant trading units, order placement system, and related communication systems, etc.

rule: 2.1.2 This exchange establishes a trading floor. Members of this exchange (hereinafter referred to as members) may submit applications through their traders stationed in the trading floor. Except with special permission from this exchange, only the following personnel are permitted to enter the trading floor: (i) registered traders; (ii) on-site supervisory personnel.
rule_cn: 2.1.2 This exchange has a trading hall. Members of this exchange (hereinafter referred to as members) may submit applications through their traders stationed in the trading hall. Except with special permission from this exchange, only the following personnel are allowed to enter the trading hall: (i) registered traders; (ii) on-site regulatory personnel.

rule: Section 2 Trading Participants and Trading Rights
rule_cn: Section 2 Trading Participants and Trading Rights

rule: 2.2.1 Members and institutions recognized by the Exchange that wish to conduct securities trading in the Exchange's market must apply to the Exchange for trading rights and become trading participants. Trading participants shall conduct securities trading through the participant trading business units established by the Exchange and comply with the relevant provisions of this rule and other business rules of the Exchange regarding securities trading business.
rule_cn: 2.2.1 Members and institutions recognized by the Exchange that wish to conduct securities trading in the Exchange's market must apply to the Exchange for trading rights and become trading participants. Trading participants shall conduct securities trading through the participant trading business units opened by the Exchange and comply with the relevant provisions of this rule and other business rules of the Exchange regarding securities trading business.

rule: 2.2.2 Participant trading business unit refers to the basic unit by which a trading participant can participate in securities trading on the Exchange, enjoy and exercise relevant trading rights, and accept the Exchange's relevant trading business management.
rule_cn: 2.2.2 Participant trading business unit refers to the basic unit by which a trading participant can participate in securities trading on the Exchange, enjoy and exercise relevant trading rights, and accept the Exchange's relevant trading business management.

rule: 2.2.3 The detailed rules for the management of participants' trading business units and trading permissions shall be separately stipulated by the Exchange.
rule_cn: 2.2.3 The detailed rules for the management of participants' trading business units and trading permissions shall be separately stipulated by the Exchange.

rule: Section 3 Trading Instruments
rule_cn: Section 3 Trading Instruments

rule: 2.3 The following securities may be listed and traded on this exchange: (i) stocks; (ii) funds; (iii) warrants; (iv) depositary receipts; (v) other trading instruments approved by the China Securities Regulatory Commission.
rule_cn: 2.3 The following securities may be listed and traded on this exchange: (i) stocks; (ii) funds; (iii) warrants; (iv) depositary receipts; and (v) other trading instruments approved by the China Securities Regulatory Commission.

rule: 2.4.1 The trading days of this exchange are Monday to Friday. The exchange market is closed on national statutory holidays and holidays announced by the exchange.
rule_cn: 2.4.1 The trading days of this exchange are Monday to Friday. The exchange market is closed on national statutory holidays and holidays announced by the exchange.

Rule 2.4.2 For trading using the competi: 15 to 9:25 on each trading day, the continuous bidding period is from 9:30 to 11:30 and from 13:00 to 14:57, and the closing call auction period is from 14:57 to 15:00. For fund trading, the opening call auction period is from 9:15 to 9:25 on each trading day, and the continuous bidding period is from 9:30 to 11:30 and from 13:00 to 15:00. This excludes securities that are suspended and then resumed trading during market hours. The Exchange may adjust trading hours as needed by market development and with the approval of the China Securities Regulatory Commission.
rule_cn: 2.4.2 For trading using the competitive bidding method, unless otherwise stipulated in these rules, the opening call auction period is from 9:15 to 9:25 on each trading day, the continuous bidding period is from 9:30 to 11:30 and from 13:00 to 14:57, and the closing call auction period is from 14:57 to 15:00. For fund trading, the opening call auction period is from 9:15 to 9:25 on each trading day, and the continuous bidding period is from 9:30 to 11:30 and from 13:00 to 15:00. This excludes securities that are suspended and then resumed trading during market hours. The Exchange may adjust trading hours as needed by market development and with the approval of the China Securities Regulatory Commission.

rule: Section 1 General Provisions
rule_cn: Section 1 General Provisions

rule: 3.1.1 After accepting a buy or sell order from an investor, a member shall report to the exchange in accordance with the content of the order and assume corresponding transaction and settlement responsibilities. If a member accepts a buy or sell order from an investor and a transaction is completed, the investor shall deliver to the member the proceeds from the sale of securities or the purchase of securities entrusted to the member, and the member shall deliver to the investor the proceeds from the sale of securities or the purchase of securities.
rule_cn: 3.1.1 After accepting a buy or sell order from an investor, a member shall report to the exchange in accordance with the content of the order and assume corresponding transaction and settlement responsibilities. If a member accepts a buy or sell order from an investor and a transaction is completed, the investor shall deliver to the member the proceeds from the sale of securities or the purchase of securities entrusted to the member, and the member shall deliver to the investor the proceeds from the sale of securities or the purchase of securities.

rule: 3.1.2 Trading participants shall send buy and sell orders to the Exchange's trading host through their relevant order placement systems, participant trading business units, and reporting channels, and complete transactions in accordance with these rules. The Exchange shall send the transaction results and other transaction records to the trading participants.
rule_cn: 3.1.2 Trading participants shall send buy and sell orders to the Exchange's trading host through their relevant order placement systems, participant trading business units, and reporting channels, and complete transactions in accordance with these rules. The Exchange shall send the transaction results and other transaction records to the trading participants.

rule: 3.1.3 Transaction participants shall properly keep their entrustment and declaration records in accordance with relevant regulations.
rule_cn: 3.1.3 Transaction participants shall properly keep their entrustment and declaration records in accordance with relevant regulations.

rule: 3.1.4 Securities purchased by investors may not be sold before settlement, except for those subject to day trading. Day trading refers to the sale of all or part of securities purchased by investors after the transaction has been confirmed, before settlement.
rule_cn: 3.1.4 Securities purchased by investors may not be sold before settlement, except for those subject to day trading. Day trading refers to the sale of all or part of securities purchased by investors after the transaction has been confirmed, before settlement.

rule: 3.1.5 The following types of funds are subject to same-day trading: (i) bond exchange-traded open-ended index funds; (ii) exchange-traded money market funds; (iii) gold exchange-traded open-ended securities investment funds; (iv) commodity futures exchange-traded open-ended index funds; (v) cross-border exchange-traded open-ended index funds; (vi) cross-border listed open-ended funds; (vii) warrants; (viii) other types of funds approved by the China Securities Regulatory Commission. The cross-border exchange-traded open-ended index funds and cross-border listed open-ended funds mentioned above are limited to open-ended funds whose tracked index constituent securities or investment targets are subject to same-day trading. B shares are subject to trading starting from the next trading day.
rule_cn: 3.1.5 The following types of funds are subject to same-day trading: (i) bond exchange-traded open-ended index funds; (ii) exchange-traded money market funds; (iii) gold exchange-traded open-ended securities investment funds; (iv) commodity futures exchange-traded open-ended index funds; (v) cross-border exchange-traded open-ended index funds; (vi) cross-border listed open-ended funds; (vii) warrants; (viii) other types of funds approved by the China Securities Regulatory Commission. The cross-border exchange-traded open-ended index funds and cross-border listed open-ended funds mentioned above are limited to open-ended funds whose tracked index constituent securities or investment targets are subject to same-day trading. B shares are subject to same-day trading starting from the next trading day.

rule: 3.1.6 As needed by the market, the Exchange may implement a primary dealer system. Specific measures will be separately stipulated by the Exchange and will take effect after being approved by the China Securities Regulatory Commission.

rule: 3.1.7 Investors participating in securities trading or related business on this exchange shall fully understand and be aware of the relevant risks, laws and regulations, and the exchange's business rules, comply with the relevant requirements for investor suitability management, and prudently judge whether to participate in securities trading or related business based on their own risk awareness and tolerance. Members shall earnestly fulfill their investor suitability management obligations, fully disclose investment risks, and guide clients to invest rationally.
rule_cn: 3.1.7 Investors participating in securities trading or related business on this exchange should fully understand and be aware of the relevant risks, laws and regulations, and the exchange's business rules, comply with the relevant requirements for investor suitability management, and prudently judge whether to participate in securities trading or related business based on their own risk awareness and tolerance. Members should earnestly fulfill their investor suitability management obligations, fully disclose investment risks, and guide clients to invest rationally.

rule: 3.1.8 Programmatic trading, which generates or issues trading instructions automatically through computer programs, shall comply with the regulations of the China Securities Regulatory Commission and report to the Exchange, and shall not affect the security of the Exchange's system or the normal trading order.
rule_cn: 3.1.8 Programmatic trading, which generates or issues trading instructions automatically through computer programs, shall comply with the regulations of the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) and be reported to the Exchange. It shall not affect the security of the Exchange's system or the normal trading order.

rule: Section Two: Delegation
rule_cn: Section Two: Delegation

rule: 3.2.1 Securities trading on this exchange operates under a designated trading system or other entrusted trading system stipulated by the exchange. The designated trading regulations do not apply to overseas investors engaging in B-share trading. Designated trading means that investors participating in securities trading on this exchange must designate a member in advance as their trustee for securities trading, and participate in securities trading on this exchange through that member.
rule_cn: 3.2.1 Securities trading on this exchange adopts a designated trading system or other entrusted trading system stipulated by the exchange. The designated trading regulations do not apply to overseas investors engaging in B-share trading. Designated trading means that investors participating in securities trading on this exchange must designate a member in advance as their trustee for securities trading, and participate in securities trading on this exchange through that member.

rule: 3.2.2 When adopting the designated trading system, investors shall sign a designated trading agreement with the designated trading member, clearly defining the rights, obligations, and responsibilities of both parties. Once the designated trading agreement is signed, the member may, upon the investor's application, submit a designated trading application to the exchange's trading system. If an investor wishes to change their designated trading, they shall express their intention to withdraw to the designated member, which will then submit a withdrawal instruction. For those meeting the conditions for withdrawal, members shall not restrict, obstruct, or delay the withdrawal process. The exchange accepts designated trading application instructions during market hours.
rule_cn: 3.2.2 When adopting the designated trading system, investors shall sign a designated trading agreement with the designated trading member, clearly defining the rights, obligations, and responsibilities of both parties. Once the designated trading agreement is signed, the member may, upon the investor's application, submit a designated trading application to the exchange's trading system. If an investor wishes to change their designated trading, they shall express their intention to withdraw to the designated member, which will then submit a withdrawal instruction. For those meeting the conditions for withdrawal, members shall not restrict, obstruct, or delay the withdrawal process. The exchange accepts designated trading application instructions during market hours.

rule: 3.2.3 Other matters concerning the designated transaction shall be handled in accordance with the relevant regulations of this Exchange.
rule_cn: 3.2.3 Other matters concerning designated transactions shall be handled in accordance with the relevant regulations of this exchange.

rule: 3.2.4 Investors who buy or sell securities shall open a securities account and a funds account, and sign a securities trading agency agreement with a member. After the agreement takes effect, the investor becomes a client of the member's brokerage business (hereinafter referred to as the client). Investors shall open securities accounts in accordance with the regulations of the securities registration and settlement institution.
rule_cn: 3.2.4 Investors who buy or sell securities shall open a securities account and a funds account, and sign a securities trading agency agreement with a member. After the agreement takes effect, the investor becomes a client of the member's brokerage business (hereinafter referred to as the client). Investors shall open securities accounts in accordance with the regulations of the securities registration and settlement institution.

rule: 3.2.5 Clients may instruct members to buy or sell securities through written, telephone, self-service terminals, internet, or other self-service methods. Telephone, self-service terminal, and internet self-service orders shall be executed in accordance with relevant regulations.
rule_cn: 3.2.5 Clients may entrust members to buy or sell securities through written, telephone, self-service terminals, internet, or other self-service methods. Telephone, self-service terminal, and internet self-service orders shall be executed in accordance with relevant regulations.

rule: 3.2.6 When a client participates in securities trading through self-service order placement, the member shall sign a self-service order placement agreement with them.
rule_cn: 3.2.6 When a client participates in securities trading through self-service order placement, the member shall sign a self-service order placement agreement with them.

rule: 3.2.7 Unless otherwise stipulated by the Exchange, a client's order shall include the following: (i) securities account number; (ii) securities code; (iii) buy/sell direction; (iv) order quantity; (v) order price; (vi) order type; and (vii) other information required by the Exchange and its members.
rule_cn: 3.2.7 Unless otherwise stipulated by the Exchange, a client's order shall include the following: (i) securities account number; (ii) securities code; (iii) buy/sell direction; (iv) order quantity; (v) order price; (vi) order type; and (vii) other information required by the Exchange and its members.

rule: 3.2.8 Clients may instruct members to buy or sell securities using limit orders or market orders. A limit order means that the client instructs the member to buy or sell securities at a specified price; the member must submit a buy order at or below the specified price, or a sell order at or above the specified price. A market order means that the client instructs the member to buy or sell securities at the market price.
rule_cn: 3.2.8 Clients may instruct members to buy or sell securities using limit orders or market orders. A limit order is an order from a client to a member to buy or sell securities at a specified price; the member must submit a buy order at or below the specified price, or a sell order at or above the specified price. A market order is an order from a client to a member to buy or sell securities at the market price.

rule: 3.2.9 Clients may cancel the unexecuted portion of their orders.
rule_cn: 3.2.9 Clients may cancel the unexecuted portion of their orders.

rule: 3.3.1 The Exchange accepts bid submissions from trading participants from 9:15 to 9:25, 9:30 to 11:30, and 13:00 to 15:00 each trading day. During the opening call auction from 9:20 to 9:25 and the closing call auction from 14:57 to 15:00 each trading day, the Exchange's trading system does not accept order cancellations; during other times when bid submissions are accepted, unexecuted bids can be cancelled. Cancellation instructions are only valid after confirmation by the Exchange's trading system. The Exchange may adjust the bid submission acceptance times when it deems necessary.
rule_cn: 3.3.1 The Exchange accepts bid submissions from trading participants from 9:15 to 9:25, 9:30 to 11:30, and 13:00 to 15:00 each trading day. Cancellation instructions are only valid after confirmation by the Exchange's trading system. The Exchange may adjust the bid submission times when it deems necessary.

rule: 3.3.4 Based on market needs, the Exchange may accept market orders in the following manner: (i) Best 5-level immediate execution with remaining cancellation order, that is, the order will be executed sequentially at the counterparty's price within the counterparty's real-time best 5 price levels, and the remaining unexecuted portion will be automatically cancelled; (ii) Best 5-level immediate execution with remaining conversion to limit order, that is, the order will be executed sequentially at the counterparty's price within the counterparty's real-time best 5 price levels, and the remaining unexecuted portion will be automatically cancelled;  Within each optimal price range, trades are executed sequentially at the counterparty's price. Any unexecuted trades are converted into limit orders at the latest executed price of the order placed by the order holder. If no trade is executed, the order is converted into a limit order at the order holder's optimal price. If no order is placed by the order holder, the order is cancelled. (iii) The order placed at the order holder's optimal price is the order price at which the order holder's optimal price appears in the centralized order book when the order enters the trading system. If no order is placed by the order holder when the order enters the trading system, the order is automatically cancelled. (iv) The order placed at the counterparty's optimal price is the order price at which the counterparty's optimal price appears in the centralized order book when the order enters the trading system. If no order is placed by the counterparty when the order enters the trading system, the order is automatically cancelled. (v) Other methods stipulated by the exchange.
rule_cn: 3.3.4 Based on market needs, the Exchange may accept market orders in the following manner: (i) Best 5-level immediate execution with remaining cancellation order, that is, the order will be executed sequentially at the counterparty's price within the top 5 price levels in real time, and the remaining unexecuted portion will be automatically cancelled; (ii) Best 5-level immediate execution with remaining conversion to limit order, that is, the order will be executed sequentially at the counterparty's price within the top 5 price levels in real time, and the remaining unexecuted portion will be automatically cancelled;  Within each optimal price range, trades are executed sequentially at the counterparty's price. Any unexecuted trades are converted into limit orders at the latest executed price of the order placed by the order holder. If no trade is executed, the order is converted into a limit order at the order holder's optimal price. If no order is placed by the order holder, the order is cancelled. (iii) The order placed at the order holder's optimal price is the order price at which the order holder's optimal price appears in the centralized order book when the order enters the trading system. If no order is placed by the order holder when the order enters the trading system, the order is automatically cancelled. (iv) The order placed at the counterparty's optimal price is the order price at which the counterparty's optimal price appears in the centralized order book when the order enters the trading system. If no order is placed by the counterparty when the order enters the trading system, the order is automatically cancelled. (v) Other methods stipulated by the exchange.

rule: 3.3.13 This Exchange applies price fluctuation limits to stock and fund trading, with a fluctuation limit of 10%. The formula for calculating the price fluctuation limit for stocks and funds is: Price fluctuation limit = Previous closing price × (1 ± Price fluctuation limit percentage). Stocks falling under any of the following circumstances are exempt from price fluctuation limits: (i) the first five trading days after an initial public offering (IPO); (ii) the first trading day of a delisted stock entering the delisting period; (iii) the first trading day of a stock relisted after delisting; (iv) other circumstances recognized by this Exchange. With the approval of the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC), this Exchange may adjust the price fluctuation limit percentage for securities.

rule: 3.3.14 For stock trading, limit orders during the continuous trading phase shall meet the following requirements for valid order price ranges: (i) the buy order price shall not exceed 102% of the buy benchmark price and the higher of the ten minimum price increments above the buy benchmark price; (ii) the sell order price shall not be lower than 98% of the sell benchmark price and the lower of the ten minimum price increments below the sell benchmark price. The buy (sell) benchmark price mentioned in the preceding paragraph is the lowest sell (highest buy) order price disclosed in real time; if there is no lowest sell (highest buy) order price disclosed in real time, it is the highest buy (lowest sell) order price disclosed in real time; if there is no highest buy (lowest sell) order price disclosed in real time, it is the latest transaction price; if there is no transaction on the day, it is the previous closing price. The provisions of the preceding two paragraphs of this article do not apply to limit orders during the temporary suspension phase of trading. The Exchange may adjust the valid order price range for stocks according to market conditions.
rule_cn: 3.3.14 For stock trading, limit orders during the continuous trading phase shall meet the following requirements for valid order price ranges: (i) The buy order price shall not exceed 102% of the buy benchmark price or the higher of the ten smallest price increments above the buy benchmark price; (ii) The sell order price shall not be lower than 98% of the sell benchmark price or the lower of the ten smallest price increments below the sell benchmark price. The buy (sell) benchmark price mentioned in the preceding paragraph is the lowest sell (highest buy) order price disclosed in real time; if there is no lowest sell (highest buy) order price disclosed in real time, it is the highest buy (lowest sell) order price disclosed in real time; if there is no highest buy (lowest sell) order price disclosed in real time, it is the latest transaction price; if there is no transaction on the day, it is the previous closing price. The provisions of the preceding two paragraphs of this article do not apply to limit orders during the temporary suspension phase of trading. The Exchange may adjust the valid order price range for stocks according to market conditions.

rule: 3.3.17 The calculation results of the price fluctuation limit and the valid bid/ask price range shall be rounded to the minimum bid/ask price increment. If the absolute value of the difference between the price fluctuation limit and the previous closing price, or the difference between the upper or lower limit of the valid bid/ask price range and the benchmark price, is lower than the minimum bid/ask price increment, the corresponding price shall be calculated by adding or subtracting one minimum bid/ask price increment from the previous closing price or the benchmark price. If the price fluctuation limit and the upper or lower limit of the valid bid/ask price range are lower than the minimum bid/ask price increment, the minimum bid/ask price increment shall be used as the corresponding price.

rule: 3.4.1 Securities auction trading adopts two methods: call auction and continuous auction. Call auction refers to an auction method in which buy and sell orders accepted within a specified time are matched all at once. Continuous auction refers to an auction method in which buy and sell orders are matched one by one continuously.
rule_cn: 3.4.1 Securities auction trading adopts two methods: call auction and continuous auction. Call auction refers to an auction method in which buy and sell orders accepted within a specified time are matched all at once. Continuous auction refers to an auction method in which buy and sell orders are matched one by one continuously.

rule: 3.4.2 Buy and sell orders that are not executed in the current auction phase will automatically proceed to the next auction phase of the day.

rule: Fifth Section Transactions
rule_cn: Fifth Section Transactions

rule: 3.5.1 Securities auction trading is matched according to the principles of price priority and time priority. The price priority principle at the time of execution is: higher-priced buy orders take precedence over lower-priced buy orders, and lower-priced sell orders take precedence over higher-priced sell orders. The time priority principle at the time of execution is: for buy and sell orders with the same direction and price, the earlier order takes precedence over the later order. The order is determined by the time the trading system accepts the orders.
rule_cn: 3.5.1 Securities auction trading is matched according to the principles of price priority and time priority. The price priority principle at the time of execution is: higher-priced buy orders take precedence over lower-priced buy orders, and lower-priced sell orders take precedence over higher-priced sell orders. The time priority principle at the time of execution is: for buy and sell orders with the same direction and price, those submitted earlier take precedence over those submitted later. The order is determined by the time the trading system accepts the orders.

rule: 3.5.2 During the call auction, the principle for determining the transaction price is as follows: (i) the price that achieves the maximum trading volume; (ii) the price at which all buy orders above the price and all sell orders below the price are executed; (iii) the price at which at least one side (buyer or seller) with the same price is fully executed. If two or more bid prices meet the above conditions, the bid price that minimizes the unexecuted volume is the transaction price; if there are still two or more bid prices that minimize the unexecuted volume and meet the above conditions, the median price is the transaction price. All transactions in the call auction are executed at the same price.
rule_cn: 3.5.2 During the call auction, the principles for determining the transaction price are: (i) the price that maximizes the trading volume; (ii) the price at which all buy orders above the price and all sell orders below the price are executed; (iii) the price at which at least one side (buyer or seller) with the same price is fully executed. If two or more bid prices meet the above conditions, the bid price that minimizes the unexecuted volume is the transaction price; if there are still two or more bid prices that minimize the unexecuted volume and meet the above conditions, the median price is the transaction price. All transactions in the call auction are executed at the same price.

rule: 3.5.3 During continuous bidding, the principle for determining the transaction price is as follows: (i) If the highest bid price is the same as the lowest ask price, the transaction price shall be the same; (ii) If the bid price is higher than the lowest ask price displayed at the moment, the transaction price shall be the lowest ask price displayed at the moment; (iii) If the ask price is lower than the highest bid price displayed at the moment, the transaction price shall be the highest bid price displayed at the moment.
rule_cn: 3.5.3 During continuous bidding, the principle for determining the transaction price is as follows: (i) If the highest bid price is the same as the lowest ask price, the transaction price shall be the same; (ii) If the bid price is higher than the lowest ask price displayed at the moment, the transaction price shall be the lowest ask price displayed at the moment; (iii) If the ask price is lower than the highest bid price displayed at the moment, the transaction price shall be the highest bid price displayed at the moment.

rule: 3.5.5 Once a buy or sell order is matched and executed by the trading system, the transaction is considered established. Transactions that comply with all provisions of this rule become effective upon establishment, and both the buyer and seller must acknowledge the transaction result and fulfill their clearing and settlement obligations. For transactions that cause serious consequences due to force majeure, unforeseen events, or unauthorized intrusion into the trading system, the Exchange may take appropriate measures or declare them invalid. For transactions that are manifestly unfair, the Exchange may take appropriate measures upon its determination. The Exchange reserves the right to cancel any transaction that violates this rule and seriously disrupts the normal operation of the securities market, and the resulting losses shall be borne by the violating trader.

rule: 3.5.6 For transactions executed in accordance with these rules, the transaction results shall be based on the transaction data recorded by the exchange's trading system.

rule: Section Six: Block Trades
rule_cn: Section Six: Block Trades

rule: 3.6.1 Securities trading conducted on this Exchange may be conducted via block trade if the following conditions are met: (i) The number of shares traded in a single transaction for A shares shall not be less than 300,000 shares, or the transaction amount shall not be less than RMB 2 million; (ii) The number of shares traded in a single transaction for B shares shall not be less than 300,000 shares, or the transaction amount shall not be less than USD 200,000; (iii) The number of units traded in a single transaction for funds shall not be less than 2 million units, or the transaction amount shall not be less than RMB 2 million; This Exchange may adjust the minimum limit for block trades according to market conditions.
rule_cn: 3.6.1 Securities trading conducted on this Exchange may be conducted via block trade if the following conditions are met: (i) The number of shares traded in a single transaction for A shares shall not be less than 300,000 shares, or the transaction amount shall not be less than RMB 2 million; (ii) The number of shares traded in a single transaction for B shares shall not be less than 300,000 shares, or the transaction amount shall not be less than USD 200,000; (iii) The number of units traded in a single transaction for funds shall not be less than 2 million units, or the transaction amount shall not be less than RMB 2 million; This Exchange may adjust the minimum limit for block trades according to market conditions.

rule: 3.6.5 Intent declaration instructions shall include securities account number, securities code, buy/sell direction, etc. Intent declarations shall be true and valid. If the price declared by the declarant is not specified, it shall be deemed that the declarant is willing to buy at least at the specified minimum price or sell at the specified maximum price; if the quantity is not specified, it shall be deemed that the declarant is willing to execute the transaction at least at the minimum order quantity for a block trade.

rule: 3.6.7 After the buyer and seller reach an agreement on a block trade, they shall entrust a member to submit a trade declaration to the exchange's trading system through the trading business unit. The declaration instruction shall include the following: (i) security code; (ii) security account; (iii) buy/sell direction; (iv) transaction price; (v) transaction quantity; (vi) other content stipulated by the exchange. The security code, transaction price, and transaction quantity in the trade declaration must be consistent.

The declaration instruction shall include the following: (i) the security code; (ii) the security account number; (iii) the buy/sell direction; (iv) the transaction price; (v) the transaction quantity; and (vi) other content stipulated by the exchange. The security code, transaction price, and transaction quantity in the transaction declaration must be consistent.

rule: 3.6.8 After the buyer and seller reach an agreement, they shall submit a transaction declaration to the exchange's trading system. The declared transaction price and quantity must be consistent. Unless otherwise stipulated by the exchange, once the transaction declaration and transaction result of a block trade are confirmed by the exchange, they cannot be withdrawn or changed. Both the buyer and seller must acknowledge the transaction result and fulfill their clearing and settlement obligations.

rule: 3.6.9 For fixed-price orders, both buyers and sellers may submit orders based on the closing price of the day's auction market or the volume-weighted average price of the day's trading. Fixed-price order instructions must include the securities account, securities code, buy/sell direction, transaction type, and transaction quantity. Fixed-price orders can be cancelled during the fixed-price order acceptance period; after the order submission period ends, the Exchange will match and execute fixed-price orders according to the time priority principle. Unexecuted portions will be automatically cancelled.
rule_cn: 3.6.9 For fixed-price orders, both buyers and sellers may submit orders based on the closing price of the day's auction market or the volume-weighted average price of the day's trading. Fixed-price order instructions must include the securities account, securities code, buy/sell direction, transaction type, and transaction quantity. Fixed-price orders can be cancelled during the fixed-price order acceptance period; after the order submission period ends, the Exchange will match and execute fixed-price orders according to the time priority principle. Unexecuted portions will be automatically cancelled.

rule: 3.6.10 For securities with price fluctuation limits, the transaction price shall be determined by the buyer and seller within the daily price fluctuation limit. For securities without price fluctuation limits, the transaction price shall not exceed the lower of 120% of the average real-time transaction price of the security in the daily auction and the highest traded price, and shall not be lower than the higher of 80% of the average real-time transaction price of the security in the daily auction and the lowest traded price. The average price is calculated as follows: Average Price = Transaction Amount / Transaction Count. The result is rounded to the minimum price fluctuation unit. Orders accepted between 16:00 and 17:00 on each trading day are subject to the price fluctuation limits accepted during other trading sessions of the same day.
rule_cn: 3.6.10 For securities with price fluctuation limits, the transaction price shall be determined by the buyer and seller within the daily price fluctuation limit. For securities without price fluctuation limits, the transaction price shall not exceed the lower of 120% of the average real-time transaction price of the security in the daily auction and the highest traded price, and shall not be lower than the higher of 80% of the average real-time transaction price of the security in the daily auction and the lowest traded price. The average price is calculated as follows: Average Price = Transaction Amount / Transaction Count. The result is rounded to the minimum price fluctuation unit. Orders accepted between 16:00 and 17:00 on each trading day are subject to the price fluctuation limits accepted during other trading sessions of the same day.

rule: 3.6.11 Members shall ensure that participants in block trades actually possess the securities or funds corresponding to their declarations. Institutions holding or leasing trading units of the Exchange participating in block trades shall submit their declarations through the trading units they hold or lease and ensure that they possess the securities or funds corresponding to their declarations.

rule: 3.6.12 Block trades are not included in the calculation of the exchange's real-time market data and indices, but the trading volume of a block trade is included in the total trading volume of that security after the block trade is completed.

rule: 4.1.3 Unless otherwise stipulated in these rules, the closing price of a security shall be determined through a closing auction. If the closing auction fails to produce a closing price or no closing auction is conducted, the closing price shall be the volume-weighted average price of all transactions in the minute preceding the last transaction of the day (including the last transaction). The closing price of a fund shall be the volume-weighted average price of all transactions in the minute preceding the last transaction of the day (including the last transaction). If there are no transactions on a given day, the previous closing price shall be used as the closing price for that day.

rule: 4.2.3 This exchange may impose a special suspension on securities suspected of illegal or irregular trading and issue a public announcement. The relevant parties shall submit a written report as required by this exchange. The timing and method of the special suspension and resumption of trading shall be determined by this exchange.
rule_cn: 4.2.3 This exchange may impose a special suspension on securities suspected of illegal or irregular trading and issue a public announcement. rule: 4.2.4 The Exchange shall implement a temporary intraday trading halt if any of the following circumstances occur in the competitive bidding of the Exchange's stocks, and the duration of a single temporary intraday trading halt shall be 10 minutes: (i) the intraday trading price of a stock without price fluctuation limits rises or falls by 30% or more from the opening price of the day for the first time; (ii) the intraday trading price of a stock without price fluctuation limits rises or falls by 60% or more from the opening price of the day for the first time; (iii) other circumstances as determined by the China Securities Regulatory Commission or the Exchange.
rule_cn: 4.2.4 The Exchange shall implement a temporary intraday trading halt if any of the following circumstances occur in the competitive bidding of stocks on this Exchange, and the duration of a single temporary intraday trading halt shall be 10 minutes: (i) the intraday trading price of a stock without price fluctuation limits rises or falls by 30% or more from the opening price of the day for the first time; (ii) the intraday trading price of a stock without price fluctuation limits rises or falls by 60% or more from the opening price of the day for the first time; (iii) other circumstances identified by the China Securities Regulatory Commission or the Exchange.

rule: 4.2.5 If a security is suspended from trading during market hours, any orders that were not executed before the suspension will participate in the trading after the security resumes trading on the same day; during the suspension period, orders can be submitted or cancelled; upon resumption of trading, accepted orders will be subject to a call auction, and the virtual reference price, virtual matched volume, and virtual unmatched volume will not be disclosed during the suspension and call auction periods. The suspension and resumption times of securities are subject to the announcement of the Exchange. If a security's suspension period extends beyond 14:57 and it must resume trading on the same day, trading will resume at 14:57, and accepted orders will be subject to a resumption call auction, followed by a closing call auction.
rule_cn: 4.2.5 If a security is suspended from trading during market hours, any orders that were not executed before the suspension will participate in the trading after the security resumes trading on the same day; during the suspension period, orders can be submitted or cancelled; upon resumption of trading, accepted orders will be subject to a call auction, and the virtual reference price, virtual matched volume, and virtual unmatched volume of the call auction will not be disclosed during the suspension and call auction periods. The suspension and resumption times of securities are subject to the announcement of the Exchange. If a security's suspension period extends beyond 14:57 and it must resume trading on the same day, trading will resume at 14:57, and accepted orders will be subject to a resumption call auction, followed by a closing call auction.

rule: Section 3 Ex-rights and Ex-dividend
rule_cn: Section 3 Ex-rights and Ex-dividend

rule: 4.3.1 When listed securities undergo rights distribution, capitalization of reserves, rights issues, or other similar events, the Exchange shall process the ex-rights and ex-dividend treatment of the securities on the trading day following the record date (the last trading day for B shares), unless otherwise stipulated by the Exchange.
rule_cn: 4.3.1 When listed securities undergo rights distribution, capitalization of reserves, rights issues, or other similar events, the Exchange shall process the ex-rights and ex-dividend treatment of the securities on the trading day following the record date (the last trading day for B shares), unless otherwise stipulated by the Exchange.

rule: 4.3.2 The formula for calculating the ex-rights (ex-dividend) reference price is: Ex-rights (ex-dividend) reference price = [(previous closing price - cash dividend) + rights issue price × change in outstanding shares] / (1 + change in outstanding shares). If the securities issuer deems it necessary to adjust the above calculation formula, it may submit an adjustment application to the Exchange and explain the reasons. The Exchange may decide to adjust the ex-rights (ex-dividend) reference price calculation formula based on the application and publish it. The previous closing price of the security displayed in the real-time market data on the ex-rights (ex-dividend) date is the ex-rights (ex-dividend) reference price.
rule_cn: 4.3.2 The formula for calculating the ex-rights (ex-dividend) reference price is: Ex-rights (ex-dividend) reference price = [(previous closing price - cash dividend) + rights issue price × change in outstanding shares] / (1 + change in outstanding shares). If the securities issuer deems it necessary to adjust the above calculation formula, it may submit an adjustment application to the Exchange and explain the reasons. The Exchange may decide to adjust the ex-rights (ex-dividend) reference price calculation formula based on the application and publish it. The previous closing price of the security displayed in the real-time market data on the ex-rights (ex-dividend) date is the ex-rights (ex-dividend) reference price.

rule: 4.3.3 For securities trading on the ex-rights (ex-dividend) date, the ex-rights (ex-dividend) reference price shall be used as the benchmark for calculating the price fluctuation range, unless otherwise stipulated by the Exchange.
rule_cn: 4.3.3 For securities trading on the ex-rights (ex-dividend) date, the ex-rights (ex-dividend) reference price shall be used as the benchmark for calculating the price fluctuation range, unless otherwise stipulated by the Exchange.

rule: Section 4 Risk Warning Board Trading Matters
rule_cn: Section 4: Trading Matters Related to the Risk Warning Board

rule: 4.4.1 The Shanghai Stock Exchange has established a Risk Warning Board for Listed Companies (hereinafter referred to as the Risk Warning Board). Main Board stocks subject to risk warnings under the 'Shanghai Stock Exchange Stock Listing Rules' (hereinafter referred to as Risk Warning Stocks), and Main Board stocks for which the Exchange has made a delisting decision but are in the delisting period and have not yet been delisted (hereinafter referred to as Delisting Stocks), shall be traded on the Risk Warning Board, and the provisions of this section shall apply. Where this section does not provide for a specific matter, these rules and other relevant provisions shall apply.
rule_cn: 4.4.1 The Shanghai Stock Exchange has established a Risk Warning Board for Listed Companies (hereinafter referred to as the Risk Warning Board). Main Board stocks subject to risk warnings under the 'Shanghai Stock Exchange Stock Listing Rules' (hereinafter referred to as Risk Warning Stocks), and Main Board stocks for which the Exchange has made a delisting decision but are in the delisting period and have not yet been delisted (hereinafter referred to as Delisting Stocks), will be traded on the Risk Warning Board, and the provisions of this section shall apply. Where this section does not provide otherwise, these rules and other relevant provisions shall apply.

rule: 4.4.2 If a listed company's stock is subject to delisting risk warning or other risk warnings, it shall be traded on the risk warning board from the date the risk warning measures are implemented until the trading day preceding the date the measures are revoked.
rule_cn: 4.4.2 If a listed company's stock is subject to delisting risk warning or other risk warnings, it shall be traded on the risk warning board from the date the risk warning measures are implemented until the trading day preceding the date the measures are revoked.

rule: 4.4.3 Stocks undergoing delisting rectification shall be traded on the risk warning board for 15 trading days from the start of the delisting rectification period. The Exchange shall delist the stock within 5 trading days after the expiration of this period, and the company's stock shall cease listing. If a delisted stock is suspended from trading for the entire day during the risk warning board period, the suspension period shall not be counted towards the 15 delisting rectification trading days stipulated in the preceding paragraph. The cumulative number of days of full-day suspension shall not exceed 5 trading days.
rule_cn: 4.4.3 Stocks undergoing delisting rectification shall be traded on the risk warning board for 15 trading days from the start of the delisting rectification period. The Exchange shall delist the stock within 5 trading days after the expiration of this period, and the company's stock shall be delisted. If a delisted stock is suspended from trading for the entire day during the risk warning board period, the suspension period shall not be counted in the 15 delisting rectification trading days stipulated in the preceding paragraph. The cumulative number of days of full-day suspension shall not exceed 5 trading days.

rule: 4.4.4 Trading information for stocks under risk warning and stocks undergoing delisting shall be disclosed separately from the trading information of other stocks. Members shall display the trading information for these two types of stocks accordingly and independently.
rule_cn: 4.4.4 Trading information for stocks under risk warning and stocks undergoing delisting shall be disclosed separately from the trading information of other stocks. Members shall display the trading information for these two types of stocks accordingly and independently.

rule: 4.4.5 Individual investors who wish to purchase delisted stocks must have at least 24 months of stock trading experience, and the average daily assets (excluding securities and funds borrowed through margin trading) in their securities and cash accounts opened in their own name must be at least RMB 500,000 for the 20 trading days prior to applying for trading access. Individual investors who do not meet the above requirements may only sell the delisted stocks they already hold.
rule_cn: 4.4.5 Individual investors who wish to purchase delisted stocks must have at least 24 months of stock trading experience, and the average daily assets (excluding securities and funds borrowed through margin trading) in their securities and cash accounts opened in their own name must be at least RMB 500,000 for the 20 trading days prior to applying for trading access. Individual investors who do not meet the above requirements may only sell the delisted stocks they already hold.

rule: 4.4.6 Members shall, in accordance with the requirements of these Measures, prudently assess the suitability of individual investors participating in the trading of delisted stocks, and shall not accept orders from investors who do not meet the suitability requirements to purchase delisted stocks. Investors shall, based on the suitability requirements stipulated in these Measures and their own risk tolerance, prudently decide whether to participate in the trading of delisted stocks, and shall not refuse to assume the responsibility for trading and settlement of delisted stocks on the grounds that they do not meet the suitability requirements.
rule_cn: 4.4.6 Members shall, in accordance with the requirements of these Measures, prudently assess the suitability of individual investors participating in the trading of delisted stocks, and shall not accept orders from investors who do not meet the suitability requirements to purchase delisted stocks. Investors shall, based on the suitability requirements stipulated in these Measures and their own risk tolerance, prudently decide whether to participate in the trading of delisted stocks, and shall not refuse to assume the responsibility for trading and settlement of delisted stocks on the grounds that they do not meet the suitability requirements.

rule: 4.4.7 Members shall require ordinary investors who are placing their first order to purchase risk-alert stocks or delisted stocks to sign a risk disclosure statement for both risk-alert stocks and delisted stocks, either in paper or electronic form. Members shall not accept purchase orders from clients who have not signed the risk disclosure statements.
rule_cn: 4.4.7 Members shall require ordinary investors who are placing their first order to purchase risk-alert stocks or delisted stocks to sign a risk disclosure statement for both risk-alert stocks and delisted stocks, either in paper or electronic form. Members shall not accept purchase orders from clients who have not signed the risk disclosure statement.

rule: 4.4.8 Members shall, through various channels such as their business premises, company websites, and trading systems, highlight the trading risks of risk-alert stocks and stocks undergoing delisting; for stocks undergoing delisting, they shall also inform clients of information such as the remaining trading days for the relevant stocks before the market opens each trading day.
rule_cn: 4.4.8 Members shall, through various channels such as their business premises, company websites, and trading systems, highlight the trading risks of risk-alert stocks and stocks undergoing delisting; for stocks undergoing delisting, they shall also inform clients of information such as the remaining trading days for the relevant stocks before the market opens each trading day.

rule: 4.4.9 Investors should use limit orders when buying or selling risk-alert stocks and stocks undergoing delisting.
rule_cn: 4.4.9 Investors should use limit orders when buying or selling risk-alert stocks and stocks undergoing delisting.

rule: 4.4.10 The daily price fluctuation limit for stocks under risk warning is 5%, and the daily price fluctuation limit for stocks undergoing delisting is 10%.

rule: 4.4.11 Risk Warning: If the cumulative deviation of the daily closing price of a stock reaches ±12% within three consecutive trading days, it is considered abnormal fluctuation. The exchange will publish the names of the five member brokerage firms with the largest cumulative buy and sell amounts during the period of abnormal fluctuation for that stock, along with their respective buy and sell amounts.
rule_cn: 4.4.11 Risk Warning: If the cumulative deviation of the daily closing price of a stock reaches ±12% within three consecutive trading days, it is considered abnormal fluctuation. The exchange will publish the names of the five member brokerage firms with the largest cumulative buy and sell amounts during the period of abnormal fluctuation for that stock, along with their respective buy and sell amounts.

rule: 4.4.12 An investor's cumulative purchase of a single risk-alert stock through competitive bidding and block trading on a given day shall not exceed 500,000 shares. The cumulative purchase quantity of risk-alert stocks by an investor on a given day shall be calculated by combining the purchase volume of the investor's securities account opened in their own name and their margin trading account; the sum of the investor's entrusted purchase quantity, the quantity already purchased on the day, and the quantity of purchase orders submitted but not yet executed or cancelled shall not exceed 500,000 shares. Share buybacks by listed companies and share increases by shareholders holding 5% or more of the shares according to disclosed shareholding increase plans are exempt from the aforementioned 500,000-share purchase limit. Members shall take effective measures to monitor the cumulative purchase quantity of a single risk-alert stock by investors on a given day; if a client is found to have violated the preceding two paragraphs, a warning and stop shall be issued, and the matter shall be reported to the Exchange in a timely manner.
rule_cn: 4.4.12 The total number of shares of a single risk-alert stock purchased by an investor in a single day through competitive bidding and block trading shall not exceed 500,000 shares. The total number of risk-alert stocks purchased by an investor in a single day shall be calculated by combining the purchase volume of the investor's securities account opened in their own name and their margin trading account; the sum of the investor's entrusted purchase quantity, the quantity already purchased on the same day, and the quantity of purchase orders submitted but not yet executed or cancelled shall not exceed 500,000 shares. Share buybacks by listed companies and share increases by shareholders holding 5% or more of the shares according to disclosed shareholding increase plans are exempt from the aforementioned 500,000-share purchase limit. Members shall take effective measures to monitor the total number of single risk-alert stocks purchased by investors in a single day; if a client is found to have violated the preceding two paragraphs, a warning and stop shall be issued, and the violation shall be reported to the Exchange in a timely manner.

rule_cn: 5.1.1 The exchange publishes real-time securities trading quotes, securities indices, and other trading information, including public securities trading information, on each trading day.

rule: 5.1.2 This exchange shall promptly compile and publish various daily, weekly, monthly, and annual reports reflecting market transaction conditions.
rule_cn: 5.1.2 This exchange shall promptly compile and publish various daily, weekly, monthly, and annual reports reflecting market transaction conditions.

rule: 5.1.3 Trading information generated by this exchange belongs to this exchange. No organization or individual may use or disseminate it without the permission of this exchange. rule: Second Section Real-Time Market Data
rule_cn: Second Section Real-Time Market Data

rule: 5.2.1 During the opening call auction period from 9:15 to 9:25 and the closing call auction period from 14:57 to 15:00 on each trading day, the real-time market information includes: security code, security abbreviation, previous closing price, virtual reference price for the call auction, virtual matched volume, and virtual unmatched volume.
rule_cn: 5.2.1 During the opening call auction period from 9:15 to 9:25 and the closing call auction period from 14:57 to 15:00 on each trading day, the real-time market data includes: security code, security abbreviation, previous closing price, virtual reference price for the call auction, virtual matched volume, and virtual unmatched volume.

rule: 5.2.2 During continuous bidding, real-time market data includes: security code, security abbreviation, previous closing price, latest transaction price, highest transaction price of the day, lowest transaction price of the day, cumulative transaction volume of the day, cumulative transaction amount of the day, the highest 5 buy orders and quantities in real time, and the lowest 5 sell orders and quantities in real time.
rule_cn: 5.2.2 During continuous bidding, real-time market data includes: security code, security abbreviation, previous closing price, latest transaction price, highest transaction price of the day, lowest transaction price of the day, cumulative transaction volume of the day, cumulative transaction amount of the day, the highest 5 buy orders and quantities in real time, and the lowest 5 sell orders and quantities in real time.

rule: 5.2.3 On the first day of listing of securities, the closing price displayed in real-time market data shall be the issue price, unless otherwise stipulated by the Exchange.

rule: Section 4 Public Information on Securities Transactions
rule_cn: Section 4 Public Information on Securities Transactions

rule: 5.4.1 In the event of any of the following circumstances in the auction trading of stocks and closed-end funds with price fluctuation limits, the Exchange shall publish the names of the top 5 member brokerage firms with the largest buy and sell amounts on that day, and their buy and sell amounts: (i) The top 5 stocks (funds) with a daily closing price fluctuation deviation of ±7%, the formula for calculating the closing price fluctuation deviation is: closing price fluctuation deviation = stock (fund) fluctuation - corresponding category index fluctuation; (ii) The top 5 stocks (funds) with a daily price volatility of 15%, the formula for calculating the price volatility is: price volatility = (highest price of the day - lowest price of the day) / lowest price of the day × 100%; (iii) The top 5 stocks (funds) with a daily turnover rate of 20%. For individual stocks (funds), the turnover rate is calculated as follows: Turnover Rate = Number of Shares Traded (Units) / Number of Unrestricted Shares (Units) × 100%. For stocks with the same closing price deviation, price amplitude, or turnover rate, the ranking is determined by trading value and then trading volume, in that order. Corresponding classification indices include the SSE A-Share Index, SSE B-Share Index, and SSE Fund Index compiled by this exchange. For stocks without price fluctuation limits as stipulated in Article 3.3.13, this exchange will publish the names of the five member brokerage firms with the largest buy and sell amounts on the first trading day, along with their buy and sell amounts.
rule_cn: 5.4.1 In the event of any of the following circumstances in the auction trading of stocks and closed-end funds with price fluctuation limits, the Exchange shall publish the names of the top 5 member brokerage firms with the largest buy and sell amounts on that day, and their buy and sell amounts: (i) The top 5 stocks (funds) with a daily closing price fluctuation deviation of ±7%, the formula for calculating the closing price fluctuation deviation is: closing price fluctuation deviation = stock (fund) fluctuation - corresponding category index fluctuation; (ii) The top 5 stocks (funds) with a daily price volatility of 15%, the formula for calculating the price volatility is: price volatility = (highest price of the day - lowest price of the day) / lowest price of the day × 100%; (iii) The top 5 stocks (funds) with a daily turnover rate of 20%. For individual stocks (funds), the turnover rate is calculated as follows: Turnover Rate = Number of Shares Traded (Units) / Number of Unrestricted Shares (Units) × 100%. For stocks with the same closing price deviation, price amplitude, or turnover rate, the ranking is determined by trading value and then trading volume, in that order. Corresponding classification indices include the SSE A-Share Index, SSE B-Share Index, and SSE Fund Index compiled by this exchange. For stocks without price fluctuation limits as stipulated in Article 3.3.13, this exchange will publish the names of the five member brokerage firms with the largest buy and sell amounts on the first trading day, along with their buy and sell amounts.

rule: 5.4.2 If any of the following situations occur in the auction trading of stocks and closed-end funds, it shall be deemed as abnormal fluctuation. The Exchange shall publish the names of the five member business departments with the largest cumulative purchase and sale amounts during the period of abnormal fluctuation of the stock or closed-end fund, and their purchase and sale amounts respectively: (i) The cumulative deviation of the daily closing price within three consecutive trading days reaches ±20%. The formula for calculating the cumulative deviation of the closing price is: Cumulative deviation of closing price = (closing price at the end of the period / closing price before the beginning of the period - 1) × 100% - (closing points at the end of the period / closing points before the beginning of the period of the corresponding index - 1) × 100%. If the security has undergone ex-rights or ex-dividend during the period, the closing price shall be adjusted accordingly; (ii) The ratio of the average daily turnover rate within three consecutive trading days to the average daily turnover rate of the previous five trading days reaches 30 times, and the stock or closed-end fund has experienced abnormal fluctuations for three consecutive trading days. (iii) Other circumstances deemed abnormal fluctuations by the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) or the Exchange. The abnormal fluctuation indicator will be recalculated from the next trading day following the Exchange's announcement or from the date of resumption of trading.

rule: 5.4.3 Any of the following situations in stock auction trading constitutes a serious abnormal fluctuation, and the Exchange will publish investor classification and trading statistics during the period of serious abnormal fluctuation: (i) Four instances of the same-direction abnormal fluctuation as stipulated in Article 4.4.11 or Article 5.4.2, Item 1 within 10 consecutive trading days; (ii) The cumulative deviation of the daily closing price change reaches +100% (-50%) within 10 consecutive trading days; (iii) The cumulative deviation of the daily closing price change reaches +200% (-70%) within 30 consecutive trading days; (iv) Other situations deemed serious abnormal fluctuations by the China Securities Regulatory Commission or the Exchange. If multiple situations of serious abnormal fluctuations occur in stock trading, the Exchange will publish them together. The serious abnormal fluctuation index will be recalculated from the next trading day following the Exchange's announcement or from the date of resumption of trading.

rule: 5.4.4 In the event of severe abnormal fluctuations in stock trading, the listed company shall promptly investigate and take corresponding measures in accordance with the listing rules. If, after investigation, the listed company finds no undisclosed material matters that should be disclosed, and is unable to provide a reasonable explanation for the abnormal fluctuations, in addition to handling the matter in accordance with the listing rules, the Exchange may, based on market conditions, strengthen the monitoring of abnormal trading and require members to take effective measures to alert clients to the risks.

rule: 6.1.1 The trading of stocks and depositary receipts listed on the Science and Technology Innovation Board of this Exchange shall be governed by the provisions of this chapter. Where this chapter does not provide for a specific matter, these rules and other relevant regulations shall apply.

rule: 6.1.2 Individual investors participating in STAR Market stock trading shall meet the following conditions: (i) the average daily assets in their securities and cash accounts for the 20 trading days prior to applying for trading access shall not be less than RMB 500,000 (excluding funds and securities borrowed through margin trading); (ii) having participated in securities trading for more than 24 months; and (iii) other conditions stipulated by the Exchange. Institutional investors participating in STAR Market stock trading shall comply with the provisions of laws and regulations and the Exchange's business rules. The Exchange may adjust the above conditions according to market conditions.

rule: 6.1.3 Members shall require ordinary investors who are placing their first order to purchase shares on the Science and Technology Innovation Board (STAR Market) to sign a risk disclosure statement for STAR Market stock trading in paper or electronic form. The risk disclosure statement shall fully disclose the main risk characteristics of the STAR Market. Members shall not accept subscription or purchase orders from clients who have not signed the risk disclosure statement.

rule: 6.1.4 Trading of stocks on the Science and Technology Innovation Board (STAR Market) may implement a market maker mechanism, whereby market makers can provide two-sided quotation services for STAR Market stocks. Market makers shall comply with laws and regulations and the relevant business rules of the Exchange, and shall undertake obligations such as providing continuous two-sided quotations and responding to two-sided quotations in accordance with the market-making agreement. The conditions, rights, obligations, and supervision and management of market makers for STAR Market stocks shall be separately stipulated by the Exchange and shall take effect upon approval by the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC).

rule: 6.1.6 The Exchange imposes price fluctuation limits on STAR Market stocks, with a limit of 20%. STAR Market stocks falling under any of the following circumstances are exempt from price fluctuation limits: (i) the first five trading days after an initial public offering (IPO); (ii) the first trading day of a delisted stock entering the delisting period; (iii) other circumstances as determined by the Exchange. The following funds listed on the Exchange are subject to a 20% price fluctuation limit: (i) exchange-traded open-ended index funds and index-based listed open-ended funds whose index constituents are solely STAR Market stocks or other stocks with a 20% price fluctuation limit; (ii) listed open-ended funds whose, according to their fund contracts, invest no less than 80% of their non-cash fund assets in STAR Market stocks or other stocks with a 20% price fluctuation limit.

rule: 6.1.8 For trading of stocks on the Science and Technology Innovation Board, limit orders during the continuous bidding phase shall meet the following requirements for valid order price ranges: (i) the buy order price shall not exceed 102% of the buy benchmark price; (ii) the sell order price shall not be lower than 98% of the sell benchmark price. The buy (sell) benchmark price mentioned above is the lowest sell (highest buy) order price disclosed in real time; if there is no lowest sell (highest buy) order price disclosed in real time, it is the highest buy (lowest sell) order price disclosed in real time; if there is no highest buy (lowest sell) order price disclosed in real time, it is the latest transaction price; if there is no transaction on the day, it is the previous closing price. There are no requirements for valid order price ranges for limit orders during the call auction phase and the suspension phase during the trading day.

If the closing price fluctuations, price volatility, or turnover rate are the same, the selection will be based on transaction amount and transaction volume in that order. The provisions of Section 5.4.1, paragraph 4, do not apply to STAR Market stocks without price fluctuation limits.

rule: 6.1.11 Any of the following situations in the competitive bidding trading of STAR Market stocks constitutes a serious abnormal fluctuation, and the Exchange will publish investor classification trading statistics and other information during the period of serious abnormal fluctuation: (i) Three instances of the same-direction abnormal fluctuation as stipulated in Article 6.1.10, Paragraph 1, within 10 consecutive trading days; (ii) The cumulative deviation of the daily closing price change reaches +100% (-50%) within 10 consecutive trading days; (iii) The cumulative deviation of the daily closing price change reaches +200% (-70%) within 30 consecutive trading days; (iv) Other situations deemed serious abnormal fluctuations by the China Securities Regulatory Commission or the Exchange. The serious abnormal fluctuation index will be recalculated from the next trading day following the Exchange's announcement or from the date of resumption of trading.

rule: 6.1.12 The closing price fluctuation deviation is the difference between the price change of an individual STAR Market stock and the price change of its corresponding benchmark index. The Exchange will assess and adjust the aforementioned benchmark index in a timely manner based on the development of the STAR Market and will announce it to the market. STAR Market stocks without price fluctuation limits are not included in the calculation of abnormal fluctuation and severe abnormal fluctuation indicators.

rule: 6.2.2 After-hours fixed-price trading refers to a trading method whereby, after the closing auction, the exchange's trading system matches closing price orders according to time priority and executes trades at the closing price of the day. The after-hours fixed-price trading period is from 15:05 to 15:30 each trading day. Stocks that are still suspended from trading at 15:00 on the same day will not participate in after-hours fixed-price trading.
rule_cn: 6.2.2 After-hours fixed-price trading refers to a trading method where, after the closing call auction, the exchange's trading system matches closing price orders according to time priority and executes trades at the closing price of the day. The after-hours fixed-price trading period is from 15:05 to 15:30 each trading day. Stocks that are still suspended from trading at 15:00 on the same day will not participate in after-hours fixed-price trading.

rule: 6.2.3 The Exchange accepts closing price orders from trading participants from 9:30 to 11:30 and from 13:00 to 15:30 each trading day. If trading is suspended during the trading day, orders can still be submitted during the suspension period. If trading resumes on the same day as the suspension, accepted orders will participate in the after-hours fixed-price trading after the stock resumes trading. If the stock is still suspended at 15:00 on the same day, the Exchange's trading system will no longer accept closing price orders, and any closing price orders accepted on that day will be invalid. Unexecuted orders can be cancelled during the order acceptance period. Cancellation instructions are only valid after confirmation by the Exchange's trading system.
rule_cn: 6.2.3 The Exchange accepts closing price orders from trading participants from 9:30 to 11:30 and from 13:00 to 15:30 each trading day. If trading is suspended during the trading day, orders can still be submitted during the suspension period. If trading resumes on the same day as the suspension, accepted orders will participate in the after-hours fixed-price trading of that stock. If the stock is still suspended at 15:00 on the same day, the Exchange's trading system will no longer accept closing price orders, and any closing price orders accepted on that day will be invalid. Unexecuted orders can be cancelled during the order acceptance period. Cancellation instructions are only valid after confirmation by the Exchange's trading system.

rule: 6.2.4 When a client buys or sells STAR Market stocks through after-hours fixed-price trading, they shall submit a closing price order to the member. The closing price order shall include: securities account number, securities code, buy/sell direction, limit price, and order quantity, etc.

rule: 6.2.5 After-hours fixed-price trading on this exchange accepts closing price orders from trading participants. Closing price orders must include the securities account number, securities code, brokerage code, buy/sell direction, limit price, and quantity. If the closing price is higher than the limit price of a closing price buy order, the buy order is invalid; if the closing price is lower than the limit price of a closing price sell order, the sell order is invalid.
rule_cn: 6.2.5 After-hours fixed-price trading on this exchange accepts closing price orders from trading participants. rule: 6.2.6 When placing buy or sell orders for STAR Market stocks through closing price orders, the quantity of a single order shall not be less than 200 shares (units) and shall not exceed 1 million shares (units). When selling, any remaining balance of less than 200 shares (units) shall be sold in a single order.
