rule: Chapter Seven: Supervision of Securities Transactions
rule_cn: Chapter Seven: Supervision of Securities Transactions

rule: 7.1 The Exchange will focus on monitoring the following matters in securities trading: (i) suspected insider trading, market manipulation, trading on undisclosed information, and other illegal or irregular activities; (ii) securities trading activities where the time, quantity, and method are restricted by laws, regulations, and the Exchange's business rules; (iii) abnormal trading activities that may affect the price or volume of securities; (iv) securities with obviously abnormal prices or volumes; and (v) other matters that the China Securities Regulatory Commission or the Exchange deems necessary to monitor.
rule_cn: 7.1 The Exchange will focus on monitoring the following matters in securities trading: (i) suspected insider trading, market manipulation, trading on undisclosed information, and other illegal or irregular activities; (ii) securities trading activities where the time, quantity, and method are restricted by laws, regulations, and the Exchange's business rules; (iii) abnormal trading activities that may affect securities trading prices or trading volumes; (iv) securities with obviously abnormal trading prices or trading volumes; and (v) other matters that the China Securities Regulatory Commission or the Exchange deems necessary to monitor.

rule: 7.2 Abnormal trading behaviors that may affect the price or volume of securities include: (i) false declarations, i.e., not for the purpose of a transaction, but through a large number of declarations and cancellations, to induce, mislead or influence the normal trading decisions of other investors; (ii) price manipulation, i.e. large declarations, continuous declarations, intensive declarations, or declarations at prices significantly deviating from the latest transaction price of securities, during which the securities trading price rises or falls significantly; (iii) maintaining the price or volume of securities, i.e. large declarations, continuous declarations, intensive declarations, to maintain the price or volume of securities at a specific state; (iv) a large number or frequent self-buying and selling, mutual counterparty transactions or reverse transactions between individual accounts, accounts actually controlled by oneself, or suspected related accounts; (v) through large declarations, continuous declarations, intensive declarations or declarations at prices significantly deviating from the latest transaction price of securities, during which the securities trading price rises or falls significantly; (vii) through large declarations, continuous declarations, intensive declarations or declarations at prices significantly deviating from the latest transaction price of securities, during which the securities trading price rises or falls significantly; (viii) through large declarations, continuous declarations, intensive declarations or declarations at prices significantly deviating from the latest transaction price of securities, during which the securities trading price rises or falls significantly; (vii ... (vi) Engaging in programmed trading by automatically generating or issuing trading instructions through computer programs, thereby affecting the security of the exchange's system or the normal trading order; (vii) Trading prices that significantly deviate from reasonable values, suspected of involving the transfer of benefits through securities trading; (viii) Conducting a large number of continuous transactions over a period of time; (ix) Using the trading of related securities or derivatives to influence securities prices, or using securities trading to influence the prices of related securities or derivatives; (x) Other abnormal trading behaviors that the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) or the exchange deems necessary to monitor closely. The exchange will conduct consolidated monitoring of single or multiple ordinary securities accounts, margin securities accounts, and other securities accounts (groups) suspected of being related, opened in the name of the investor or actually controlled by the same investor.
rule_cn: 7.2 Abnormal trading behaviors that may affect securities trading prices or trading volumes include: (i) false declarations, i.e., not for the purpose of a transaction, but through a large number of declarations and cancellations, to induce, mislead or influence other investors' normal trading decisions; (ii) price manipulation, i.e. large declarations, continuous declarations, intensive declarations, or declarations at prices significantly deviating from the latest transaction price of securities, during which the securities trading price rises or falls significantly; (iii) maintaining securities trading prices or trading volumes, i.e. large declarations, continuous declarations, intensive declarations, to maintain securities trading prices or trading volumes at a specific state; (iv) large or frequent self-buying and selling, mutual counterparty transactions or reverse transactions between individual accounts, accounts actually controlled by oneself, or suspected related accounts; (v) large declarations, continuous declarations, intensive declarations or declarations at prices significantly deviating from the latest transaction price of securities, during which the securities trading price rises or falls significantly; (vii) through large declarations, continuous declarations, intensive declarations or declarations at prices significantly deviating from the latest transaction price of securities, during which the securities trading price rises or falls significantly; (viii) through large declarations, continuous declarations, intensive declarations or declarations at prices significantly deviating from the latest transaction price of securities, during which the securities trading price rises or falls significantly; (vii ... (vi) Engaging in programmed trading by automatically generating or issuing trading instructions through computer programs, thereby affecting the security of the exchange's system or the normal trading order; (vii) Trading prices that significantly deviate from reasonable values, suspected of involving the transfer of benefits through securities trading; (viii) Conducting a large number of continuous transactions over a period of time; (ix) Using the trading of related securities or derivatives to influence securities prices, or using securities trading to influence the prices of related securities or derivatives; (x) Other abnormal trading behaviors that the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) or the exchange deems necessary to monitor closely. The exchange will conduct consolidated monitoring of single or multiple ordinary securities accounts, margin securities accounts, and other securities accounts (groups) suspected of being related, opened in the name of the investor or actually controlled by the same investor.

rule: 7.3 Securities with obviously abnormal trading prices or trading volumes include: (i) securities whose trading prices have risen or fallen sharply for a continuous period, or have remained in a specific state, and have deviated significantly from the rise or fall of the relevant index during the same period; (ii) securities that have been bought or sold in large quantities by the same securities business department, securities business departments in the same region, or accounts suspected of being related; (iii) other securities that the China Securities Regulatory Commission or the Exchange deems necessary to monitor closely.
rule_cn: 7.3 Securities with obviously abnormal trading prices or trading volumes include: (i) securities whose trading prices have risen or fallen sharply for a continuous period, or have remained in a specific state, and have deviated significantly from the rise or fall of the relevant index during the same period; (ii) securities that have been bought or sold in large quantities by the same securities business department, securities business departments in the same region, or accounts suspected of being related; and (iii) other securities that the China Securities Regulatory Commission or the Exchange deems necessary to monitor closely.

rule: 7.4 This exchange may, as needed by the market, collaborate with other securities and futures exchanges and other institutions to investigate situations such as those described in Article 7.2, Item 9.
rule_cn: 7.4 This exchange may, as needed by the market, collaborate with other securities and futures exchanges and other institutions to investigate situations such as those described in Article 7.2, Item 9.

rule: 7.5 Members shall earnestly fulfill their responsibilities in managing client trading activities and monitor clients' securities trading activities. If any abnormalities are discovered in a client's trading activities, the member shall promptly inform, remind, and warn the client. For abnormal trading activities that may seriously affect the order of securities trading or are suspected of being illegal or irregular, the member shall refuse to accept the client's entrustment in accordance with the securities trading agency agreement and promptly report to the exchange.
rule_cn: 7.5 Members shall earnestly fulfill their responsibilities in managing client trading activities and monitor clients' securities trading activities. If any abnormalities are discovered in a client's trading activities, the member shall promptly inform, remind, and warn the client. For abnormal trading activities that may seriously affect the order of securities trading or are suspected of being illegal or irregular, the member shall refuse to accept the client's entrustment in accordance with the securities trading agency agreement and promptly report to the exchange.

rule: 7.6 The Exchange may conduct on-site or off-site investigations into key monitoring matters in securities trading, and may require relevant members and their business departments, other trading participants or investors to provide investor account opening information, power of attorney, fund deposit and withdrawal vouchers, fund account information, relevant transaction information, etc.
rule_cn: 7.6 This exchange may conduct on-site or off-site investigations into key monitoring matters in securities trading, and may require relevant members and their business departments, other trading participants or investors to provide investor account opening information, power of attorney, fund deposit and withdrawal vouchers, fund account information, relevant transaction information, etc.

rule: 7.7 Members and their business departments, other trading participants, and investors shall cooperate with the Exchange in conducting relevant investigations and provide relevant documents and materials in a timely, truthful, accurate, and complete manner.
rule_cn: 7.7 Members and their business departments, other trading participants, and investors shall cooperate with the Exchange in conducting relevant investigations and provide relevant documents and materials in a timely, truthful, accurate, and complete manner.

rule: 7.8 For the behaviors listed in the key monitoring items in Article 7.1, the Exchange may take the following measures as appropriate: (i) verbal warning; (ii) written warning; (iii) regulatory interview; (iv) listing the account as a key monitoring account; (v) requiring investors to submit a compliance trading commitment letter; (vi) suspending investor account trading; (vii) suspending trading by the Stock Exchange Securities Trading Service Company; (viii) restricting investor account trading; (ix) other self-regulatory measures stipulated by the Exchange. If there is any objection to the measures in item (viii) above, an application for review may be submitted to the Exchange. The execution of the relevant measures will not be suspended during the review period. The Exchange will promptly report any suspected insider trading, market manipulation, trading on undisclosed information, or other illegal or irregular behaviors discovered during trading monitoring to the China Securities Regulatory Commission for investigation and handling.
rule_cn: 7.8 For the behaviors listed in the key monitoring items in Article 7.1, the Exchange may take the following measures as appropriate: (i) verbal warning; (ii) written warning; (iii) regulatory interview; (iv) listing the account as a key monitoring account; (v) requiring investors to submit a compliance trading commitment letter; (vi) suspending investor account trading; (vii) suspending trading by the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong Securities Trading Service Company; (viii) restricting investor account trading; (ix) other self-regulatory measures stipulated by the Exchange. If there is any objection to the measures in item (viii) above, an application for review may be submitted to the Exchange. The execution of the relevant measures will not be suspended during the review period. The Exchange will promptly report any suspected insider trading, market manipulation, trading on undisclosed information, or other illegal or irregular behaviors discovered during trading monitoring to the China Securities Regulatory Commission for investigation and handling.

rule: Chapter 8 Handling Abnormal Transaction Situations
rule_cn: Chapter 8 Handling Abnormal Transaction Situations

rule: 8.1 If any of the following unforeseen events prevent the normal conduct of some or all securities transactions, the Exchange may decide to take measures such as technical suspension or temporary market closure in order to maintain the normal order of securities trading and market fairness: (i) force majeure; (ii) unforeseen events; (iii) major technical failures; (iv) major human errors; (v) other abnormal circumstances as determined by the Exchange. If the unforeseen events stipulated in the preceding paragraph cause a major abnormality in the securities trading results, and settlement based on the trading results would have a significant impact on the normal order of securities trading and market fairness, the Exchange may take measures such as canceling the transaction or notifying the securities registration and settlement institution to suspend settlement.
rule_cn: 8.1 In order to maintain the normal order of securities trading and market fairness, the Exchange may decide to take measures such as technical suspension or temporary market closure if the following unforeseen events cause some or all securities trading to be unable to proceed normally: (i) force majeure; (ii) unforeseen events; (iii) major technical failures; (iv) major human errors; (v) other abnormal circumstances as determined by the Exchange. If the unforeseen events stipulated in the preceding paragraph cause a major abnormality in the securities trading results, and settlement based on the trading results would have a significant impact on the normal order of securities trading and market fairness, the Exchange may take measures such as canceling the trading or notifying the securities registration and settlement institution to suspend settlement.

rule: 8.2 If the number of member business departments experiencing market data transmission interruptions or inability to submit orders exceeds 10% of the total number of business departments, the exchange may implement a temporary market suspension.
rule_cn: 8.2 If the number of member business departments experiencing market data transmission interruptions or inability to submit orders exceeds 10% of the total number of business departments, the exchange may implement a temporary market suspension.

rule: 8.3 If the Exchange believes that an abnormal trading situation as stipulated in Articles 8.1 and 8.2 may occur and that would seriously affect the normal conduct of trading, it may decide to suspend trading technically or temporarily suspend the market.
rule_cn: 8.3 If the Exchange believes that an abnormal trading situation as stipulated in Articles 8.1 and 8.2 may occur and that would seriously affect the normal conduct of trading, it may decide to suspend trading technically or temporarily suspend the market.

rule: 8.4 This exchange will announce its decisions regarding technical suspensions, temporary market closures, cancellations of transactions, and notifications to securities registration and settlement institutions to postpone settlement, and will promptly report to the China Securities Regulatory Commission.
rule_cn: 8.4 This exchange will issue announcements regarding its decisions on technical suspensions, temporary market closures, cancellations of transactions, and notifications to securities registration and settlement institutions to postpone settlement, and will promptly report to the China Securities Regulatory Commission.

rule: 8.5 Once the reasons for a technical suspension or temporary market halt have been eliminated, the Exchange may decide to resume trading and will issue a public announcement.
rule_cn: 8.5 Once the reasons for a technical suspension or temporary market halt have been eliminated, the Exchange may decide to resume trading and will issue a public announcement.

rule: 8.6 The Exchange will monitor securities trading for risks. In the event of significant abnormal fluctuations, the Exchange may take measures such as restricting trading or forcibly suspending trading, and will report to the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC); if the fluctuations seriously affect the stability of the securities market, the Exchange may take measures such as temporary market closure and issue a public announcement. Specific measures will be stipulated separately by the Exchange.
rule_cn: 8.6 This exchange will monitor the risks of securities trading. In the event of significant abnormal fluctuations, this exchange may take measures such as restricting trading or forcibly suspending trading, and will report to the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC); if the fluctuations seriously affect the stability of the securities market, this exchange may take measures such as temporary market closure and issue a public announcement. Specific measures will be stipulated separately by this exchange.

rule: 8.7 Except for special circumstances recognized by the Exchange, if trading resumes on the same day after a technical suspension or temporary market closure, orders already accepted by the trading system before the technical suspension or temporary market closure shall remain valid. The trading system shall continue to accept orders during the technical suspension or temporary market closure period, and shall conduct call auction trading on accepted orders upon resumption of trading.
rule_cn: 8.7 Except for special circumstances recognized by the Exchange, if trading resumes on the same day after a technical suspension or temporary market closure, orders already accepted by the trading system before the technical suspension or temporary market closure are valid. The trading system continues to accept orders during the technical suspension or temporary market closure period, and will conduct call auction trading on accepted orders upon resumption of trading.

rule: 8.8 The Exchange shall not be liable for civil damages for losses caused by abnormal trading conditions, significant abnormal fluctuations, or the corresponding measures taken by the Exchange, except in cases of gross negligence.
rule_cn: 8.8 The Exchange shall not be liable for civil damages for losses caused by abnormal trading conditions, significant abnormal fluctuations, or the corresponding measures taken by the Exchange, except in cases of gross negligence.

rule: Chapter Nine: Transaction Disputes
rule_cn: Chapter Nine: Transaction Disputes

rule: 9.1 In the event of a transaction dispute between members or between a member and a client, the relevant members shall record the relevant information for the Exchange's review. If the transaction dispute affects normal trading, the member shall promptly report it to the Exchange.
rule_cn: 9.1 In the event of transaction disputes between members or between members and clients, the relevant members shall record the relevant information for the Exchange's review. If a transaction dispute affects normal trading, the member shall promptly report it to the Exchange.

rule: 9.2 In the event of a transaction dispute between trading participants or between members and clients, the Exchange may provide necessary transaction data in accordance with relevant regulations.
rule_cn: 9.2 In the event of a transaction dispute between trading participants or between members and clients, the Exchange may provide necessary transaction data in accordance with relevant regulations.

rule: 9.3 If a customer has any questions about a transaction, the member shall coordinate and resolve them.
rule_cn: 9.3 If a customer has any questions about a transaction, the member shall coordinate and resolve them.

rule: Chapter 10 Transaction Fees
rule_cn: Chapter 10 Transaction Fees

rule: 10.1 When an investor completes a transaction involving securities, they shall pay a commission to the member who conducted the securities transaction on their behalf, as stipulated.
rule_cn: 10.1 When an investor completes a transaction involving securities, they shall pay a commission to the member who conducted the securities transaction on their behalf, as stipulated.

rule: 10.2 Trading participants shall pay transaction handling fees and other charges to the Exchange as stipulated; members shall also pay membership fees to the Exchange as stipulated.
rule_cn: 10.2 Trading participants shall pay transaction handling fees and other charges to the Exchange as required; members shall also pay membership fees to the Exchange as required.

rule: 10.3 The fee items, fee standards, and management methods for securities transactions shall be implemented in accordance with relevant regulations.
rule_cn: 10.3 The fee items, fee standards, and management methods for securities transactions shall be implemented in accordance with relevant regulations.

rule: Chapter Eleven Disciplinary Actions
rule_cn: Chapter Eleven: Disciplinary Actions

rule: 11.1 If a member or other trading participant violates this rule, the Exchange shall order them to rectify the violation and, depending on the severity of the circumstances, impose one or more penalties: (i) a public reprimand; (ii) a public censure; (iii) suspension or restriction of trading privileges; (iv) cancellation of trading participant status; (v) cancellation of membership.
rule_cn: 11.1 If a member or other trading participant violates these rules, the Exchange shall order them to rectify the violation and, depending on the severity of the circumstances, impose one or more penalties: (i) a public reprimand; (ii) a public censure; (iii) suspension or restriction of trading privileges; (iv) cancellation of trading participant status; (v) cancellation of membership.

rule: 11.2 If a member or other trading participant has any objection to the disciplinary actions in items two, three, four, and five of the preceding article, they may apply to the Board of Directors of the Exchange for review within 15 days from the date of receiving the disciplinary notice. The execution of the relevant disciplinary action will not be suspended during the review period.
rule_cn: 11.2 If a member or other trading participant has any objection to the disciplinary actions in items two, three, four, and five of the preceding article, they may apply for a review to the Board of Directors of the Exchange within 15 days from the date of receiving the disciplinary notice. The execution of the relevant disciplinary action will not be suspended during the review period.

rule: Chapter Twelve Supplementary Provisions
rule_cn: Chapter Twelve Supplementary Provisions

rule: 12.1 Other trading matters related to exchange-traded funds (ETFs), warrants, and other similar instruments shall be separately stipulated by the Exchange.
rule_cn: 12.1 Other trading matters related to exchange-traded funds (ETFs), warrants, and other similar instruments shall be separately stipulated by the Exchange.

rule: 12.2 The time mentioned in these rules shall be based on the time of the exchange's trading system.
rule_cn: 12.2 The time mentioned in these rules shall be based on the time of the exchange's trading system.

rule: 12.3 In the event of any inconsistency between the Exchange's regulations concerning abnormal fluctuations in stock and fund trading and these rules, these rules shall prevail.
rule_cn: 12.3 In the event of any inconsistency between the Exchange's regulations concerning abnormal fluctuations in stock and fund trading and these rules, these rules shall prevail.

rule: 12.4 The following terms in this rule have the following meanings: (i) Market: refers to the securities trading market established by this exchange; (ii) Listed trading: refers to securities listed and traded on this exchange; (iii) Order: refers to the act of an investor authorizing a member to buy or sell securities; (iv) Order: refers to the act of a member sending a securities buy or sell instruction to the exchange's trading system; (v) Best price: refers to the highest price for buyers or the lowest price for sellers in the centralized order book. The centralized order book refers to all unexecuted order queues in the trading system at a certain point in time, arranged in order of buy/sell direction and price priority and time priority; (vi) Virtual reference price for call auction: refers to the price at which all valid orders are virtually executed according to the call auction rules and immediately disclosed as of the time of disclosure; (vii) Virtual matched quantity: refers to the quantity of orders that are virtually executed according to the virtual reference price for call auction and immediately disclosed as of the time of disclosure; (viii) Virtual unmatched quantity: refers to the remaining quantity of buy or sell orders that cannot be virtually executed according to the virtual reference price for call auction and immediately disclosed as of the time of disclosure.
rule_cn: 12.4 The following terms in these rules have the following meanings: (i) Market: refers to the securities trading market established by this exchange; (ii) Listed trading: refers to securities listed and traded on this exchange; (iii) Order: refers to the act of an investor authorizing a member to buy or sell securities; (iv) Order: refers to the act of a member sending a securities buy or sell instruction to the exchange's trading system; (v) Best price: refers to the highest price for buyers or the lowest price for sellers in the centralized order book. The centralized order book refers to all unexecuted order queues in the trading system at a certain point in time, arranged in order of buy/sell direction and price priority and time priority; (vi) Virtual reference price for call auction: refers to the price at which all valid orders are virtually executed according to the call auction rules and immediately disclosed as of the time of disclosure; (vii) Virtual matched quantity: refers to the quantity of orders that are virtually executed according to the virtual reference price for call auction and immediately disclosed as of the time of disclosure; (viii) Virtual unmatched quantity: refers to the remaining quantity of buy or sell orders that cannot be virtually executed according to the virtual reference price for call auction and immediately disclosed as of the time of disclosure.

rule: 12.5 In this rule, the terms exceeding, below, and insufficient do not include the number itself, while reaching, above, and below include the number itself.
rule_cn: 12.5 In this rule, the terms exceeding, below, and insufficient do not include the number itself, while reaching, above, and below include the number itself.

rule: 12.6 This rule shall come into effect upon approval by the Board of Directors of this Exchange and the China Securities Regulatory Commission, and the same applies to any amendments.
rule_cn: 12.6 This rule shall come into effect upon approval by the Board of Directors of this Exchange and the China Securities Regulatory Commission, and the same applies to any amendments.
